MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, as amended by AB 3078, Chapter 171, Statutes of 2004, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. As this motion was filed on July 12, 2005, the new provisions apply. The first day this motion could be noticed for is August 3, 2005.
